title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
summary: Kobie Crawford from Snorkel demonstrates that a 4B model finetuned with reinforcement learning to use proper tool discipline outperforms Qwen 3 235B on structured query tasks, with the 4B model self-correcting errors while the larger model hallucinated. The talk emphasizes that tool discipline and behavioral training matter more than raw reasoning capacity for certain task classes, and that rubric-based evaluation helps target specific training interventions.
writeup: Kobie Crawford of Snorkel argues that behavioral training, not parameter count, determines performance on certain task classes, demonstrating a 4B model finetuned with reinforcement learning to enforce tool discipline that outperforms Qwen 3 235B on structured query tasks. The smaller model self-corrects its errors while the larger one hallucinates, illustrating that disciplined tool use can beat raw reasoning capacity. The talk presents rubric-based evaluation as a method for targeting specific training interventions.
